heading: Infrastructure planning for micro and small modular nuclear reactors
label: 18751 Infrastructure planning for micro and small modular nuclear reactors

children.heading: Advanced nuclear reactor
children.label: 1 Advanced nuclear reactor
children.text: The term “advanced nuclear reactor” has the meaning given the term in .section 16271(b) of this title

children.heading: Isolated community
children.label: 2 Isolated community
children.text: The term “isolated community” has the meaning given the term in .section 17392(a) of this title

children.heading: Micro-reactor
children.label: 3 Micro-reactor
children.text: The term “micro-reactor” means an advanced nuclear reactor that has an electric power production capacity that is not greater than 50 megawatts.

children.heading: National Laboratory
children.label: 4 National Laboratory
children.text: The term “National Laboratory” has the meaning given the term in .section 15801 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: with a rated capacity of less than 300 electrical megawatts; and

children.label: B
children.text: that can be constructed and operated in combination with similar reactors at a single site.

children.heading: Report
children.label: b Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate and the Committees on Energy and Commerce and Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives a report that describes how the Department could enhance energy resilience and reduce carbon emissions with the use of micro-reactors and small modular reactors.November 15, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: the need to provide uninterrupted power to facilities of the Department for at least 3 days during power grid failures;

children.label: B
children.text: the need for protection against cyber threats and electromagnetic pulses; and

children.label: C
children.text: resilience to extreme natural events, including earthquakes, volcanic activity, tornados, hurricanes, floods, tsunamis, lahars, landslides, seiches, a large quantity of snowfall, and very low or high temperatures.
A strategy of the Department for using nuclear energy to meet resilience and carbon reduction goals of facilities of the Department.
A strategy to partner with private industry to develop and deploy micro-reactors and small modular reactors to remote communities in order to replace diesel generation and other fossil fuels.

An assessment by the Department of the value associated with enhancing the resilience of a facility of the Department by transitioning to power from micro-reactors and small modular reactors and to co-located nuclear facilities with the capability to provide dedicated power to the facility of the Department during a grid outage or failure.

children.label: A
children.text: for deploying a micro-reactor and a small modular reactor to produce energy for use by a facility of the Department in the United States by 2026;

children.label: B
children.text: for deploying a small modular reactor to produce energy for use by a facility of the Department in the United States by 2029; and

children.label: C
children.text: to include micro-reactors and small modular reactors in the planning for meeting future facility energy needs.

children.heading: Financial and technical assistance for siting micro-reactors, small modular reactors, and advanced nuclear reactors
children.label: d Financial and technical assistance for siting micro-reactors, small modular reactors, and advanced nuclear reactors

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall offer financial and technical assistance to entities to conduct feasibility studies for the purpose of identifying suitable locations for the deployment of micro-reactors, small modular reactors, and advanced nuclear reactors in isolated communities.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 2 Requirement
children.text: Prior to providing financial and technical assistance under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall conduct robust community engagement and outreach for the purpose of identifying levels of interest in isolated communities.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall not disburse more than 50 percent of the amounts available for financial assistance under this subsection to the National Laboratories.
